# RQ-0818 Phase 1 Plan: Adopt `cargo-nextest` in the local CI gate

## Task Goal
Reduce local CI/test wall-clock time by making `make test` run non-doc tests with `cargo nextest run` while preserving full coverage guarantees (including doc tests) and providing a clear, safe fallback path.

## Why This Matters
- The current `make test` runs two separate `cargo test` passes (`--all-targets` and `--doc`), which repeats test discovery/execution work and slows the required local CI loop.
- `make ci` and `make macos-ci` both depend on `test`, so improving this path directly improves developer throughput.
- `cargo-nextest` is purpose-built for faster Rust test execution and better failure UX, but it does not execute doc tests; doc-test parity must remain explicit.

## Current Reality (2026-02-12)
- `Makefile` `test` target currently runs:
  - `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --locked -- --include-ignored`
  - `cargo test --workspace --doc --locked -- --include-ignored`
- `Makefile` `ci` dependency chain is contract-sensitive and currently includes `... lint test build generate install`.
- `crates/ralph/tests/makefile_ci_contract_test.rs` enforces CI ordering and macOS preflight invariants.
- Docs in `AGENTS.md` and `GEMINI.md` describe `make test` as workspace unit+doc tests and must stay accurate.
- Official nextest docs confirm:
  - primary invocation: `cargo nextest run`
  - doctests are not currently supported and must remain in a separate `cargo test --doc` pass.

## Constraints and Invariants
- Keep CI dependency ordering unchanged unless strictly required.
- Preserve temp-dir isolation behavior (`target/tmp/ralph-ci-tmp`) and existing log-on-failure ergonomics.
- Keep explicit doc-test coverage in `make test`.
- Add a clear fallback when nextest is unavailable.
- Update all affected contract/documentation touchpoints in the same change.
- No backward-compatibility shim requirement beyond a clear fallback path.

## Implementation Directive (Phase 2)
1. Update `Makefile` test execution path.
- Keep the current temp directory setup/cleanup and log capture structure.
- Replace the non-doc test pass with nextest by default.
- Keep doc tests on `cargo test --doc`.
- Add explicit fallback behavior when `cargo-nextest` is missing.

Suggested recipe shape (adapt exactly to surrounding Makefile style):

```makefile
test:
	@echo "→ Running tests..."
	@repo_root="$$(pwd -P)"; \
	system_tmp="$${TMPDIR:-/tmp}"; \
	# ... keep existing tmp-dir setup/trap/exports ... \
	unit_log="$$run_dir/unit-tests.log"; \
	doc_log="$$run_dir/doc-tests.log"; \
	if cargo nextest --version >/dev/null 2>&1; then \
		echo "  → Using cargo-nextest for non-doc tests"; \
		if cargo nextest run --workspace --all-targets --locked -- --include-ignored >"$$unit_log" 2>&1; then \
			grep -E "^(test result:|running|     Running|Summary|Fail)" "$$unit_log" || true; \
		else \
			echo "  ✗ Workspace tests failed!"; echo ""; echo "=== Full test output ==="; cat "$$unit_log"; exit 1; \
		fi; \
	else \
		echo "  ⚠ cargo-nextest not found; falling back to cargo test --workspace --all-targets"; \
		echo "    Install with: cargo install cargo-nextest --locked"; \
		if cargo test --workspace --all-targets --locked -- --include-ignored >"$$unit_log" 2>&1; then \
			grep -E "^(test result:|running|     Running)" "$$unit_log" || true; \
		else \
			echo "  ✗ Workspace tests failed!"; echo ""; echo "=== Full test output ==="; cat "$$unit_log"; exit 1; \
		fi; \
	fi; \
	if cargo test --workspace --doc --locked -- --include-ignored >"$$doc_log" 2>&1; then \
		grep -E "^(test result:|running|     Running)" "$$doc_log" || true; \
	else \
		echo "  ✗ Doc tests failed!"; echo ""; echo "=== Full test output ==="; cat "$$doc_log"; exit 1; \
	fi; \
	echo "  ✓ Tests passed"
```

2. Update Makefile help text to reflect the new semantics.
- Change the `make test` help line to explicitly mention nextest + doc tests + fallback.

Example wording:

```makefile
@echo "  make test         # Nextest workspace tests + cargo doc tests (auto-fallback if nextest missing)"
```

3. Preserve CI gate shape.
- Keep `ci:` and `macos-ci:` dependency ordering intact.
- Do not remove existing quality gates (`check-env-safety`, `check-backup-artifacts`, `format`, `type-check`, `lint`, etc.).

4. Strengthen/adjust Makefile contract tests.
- In `crates/ralph/tests/makefile_ci_contract_test.rs`, add explicit assertions for the `test` target behavior so future edits cannot silently drop nextest or doc tests.

Suggested test addition:

```rust
#[test]
fn test_makefile_test_target_uses_nextest_and_keeps_doc_tests() -> Result<()> {
    let manifest_dir = std::path::PathBuf::from(env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR"));
    let repo_root = manifest_dir
        .parent()
        .and_then(|p| p.parent())
        .context("resolve repo root")?;
    let makefile = std::fs::read_to_string(repo_root.join("Makefile")).context("read Makefile")?;

    let test_block = extract_target_block(&makefile, "test").context("extract test block")?;
    assert!(
        test_block.contains("cargo nextest run --workspace --all-targets --locked -- --include-ignored"),
        "test target should run cargo-nextest for non-doc tests"
    );
    assert!(
        test_block.contains("cargo test --workspace --doc --locked -- --include-ignored"),
        "test target should keep explicit doc test coverage"
    );

    Ok(())
}
```

5. Update docs and guidance that describe `make test` behavior.
- Required files to update if the wording exists there:
  - `AGENTS.md`
  - `GEMINI.md`
  - `CONTRIBUTING.md`
  - `docs/cli.md` (if this change introduces/updates developer workflow references)
- Keep direct `cargo test -p ralph` examples where they represent intentional crate-level workflows; only update statements describing the default CI gate semantics.

Suggested wording pattern:
- "`make test` runs `cargo nextest run` for workspace tests, then runs `cargo test --doc` for doctests."
- "If `cargo-nextest` is unavailable, `make test` falls back to `cargo test --workspace --all-targets` and prints install guidance."

6. Validate behavior and parity.
- Run and pass:
  - `make test`
  - `make macos-ci`
- Confirm:
  - doc tests still run explicitly,
  - CI dependency ordering did not regress,
  - fallback path message is actionable,
  - no warnings/errors remain.
- Capture before/after wall-clock timing evidence (at least one local comparison) in task notes/PR description.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `make test` defaults to `cargo nextest run` for non-doc tests.
- `make test` still runs `cargo test --workspace --doc`.
- Clear fallback exists when nextest is missing and points to an install command.
- CI dependency chain remains intact.
- Contract tests enforce new `test` target expectations.
- Documentation matches actual behavior.
- `make macos-ci` passes cleanly.

## Adaptation Rule for Phase 2 Agent
If file paths, helper names, or exact text differ from this directive, preserve the intent and invariants above while adapting to the repository’s actual structure. Do not stop at listed files only; update any additional touched references discovered during implementation so behavior and documentation stay consistent.
